close_keepass_database.name: close_keepass_database
close_keepass_database.doc: Closes the currently open KeePass database.

get_kdf_algorithm.name: get_kdf_algorithm
get_kdf_algorithm.doc: Returns the key transformation algorithm used.

get_version.name: get_version
get_version.doc:
Returns the version of the KeePass database loaded with `Open Keepass Database`
        | =Return=   | =Description= |
        | ``(3, 1)`` | KDBX v3       |
        | ``(4, 0)`` | KDBX v4       |

open_keepass_database.name: open_keepass_database
open_keepass_database.doc:
Opens the specified KeePass database ``filename`` using the credentials provided.

        The ``filename`` argument specifies the location of the KeePass database

        | =Parameter=        | =Description=                              |
        | ``filename``       | specifies the path of the KeePass database |
        | ``keyfile``        | specifies the path of the keyfile          |
        | ``tranformed_key`` | specifies the transformed key              |

        Examples:
        | `Open Keepass Database` | pathtokeepassdatabase | password=mypassword   |                       |
        | `Open Keepass Database` | pathtokeepassdatabase | keyfile=pathtokeyfile |                       |
        | `Open Keepass Database` | pathtokeepassdatabase | password=mypassword   | keyfile=pathtokeyfile |

save_keepass_database.name: save_keepass_database
save_keepass_database.doc:
Save the content of the currently open KeePass database.

        | =Parameter=        | =Description=                              |
        | ``filename``       | specifies the path of the KeePass database |
        | ``tranformed_key`` | specifies the location of the keyfile      |

save_xml.name: save_xml
save_xml.doc:
Save the content of the database to a the specified file.
           NOTE: The resulting file is unencrypted!!!

        | =Parameter= | =Description=                      |
        | ``outfile`` | specifies the path of the XML file |

entry_should_be_expired.name: entry_should_be_expired
entry_should_be_expired.doc:
Fails if the specified entry is not expired.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =              | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | Set Entry Expires       | ${entry}               | ${TRUE}                 |
        | Entry Should Be Expired | ${entry}                                         |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

entry_should_not_be_expired.name: entry_should_not_be_expired
entry_should_not_be_expired.doc:
Fails if the specified entry is expired.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | Set Entry Expires           | ${entry}               | ${TRUE}                 |
        | Entry Should Not Be Expired | ${entry}                                         |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_entry_accessed_time.name: get_entry_accessed_time
get_entry_accessed_time.doc:
Return accessed time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
        object of the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | `Get Entries By Title`    | root_entry | first=True   |
        | ${value} = | `Get Entry Accessed Time` | ${entry}   | timezone=UTC |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_entry_created_time.name: get_entry_created_time
get_entry_created_time.doc:
Return created time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
        object of the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Entry Created Time` | ${entry}                |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_entry_custom_properties.name: get_entry_custom_properties
get_entry_custom_properties.doc:
Return a dictonary with all custom properties of the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | `Get Entries By Title`        | root_entry | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Entry Custom Properties` | ${entry}                |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_entry_custom_property.name: get_entry_custom_property
get_entry_custom_property.doc:
Return the value from a custom property matching the given ``key`` of the supplied ``entry``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | `Get Entries By Title`      | root_entry | first=True       |
        | ${value} = | `Get Entry Custom Property` | ${entry}   | foobar_attribute |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_entry_expired.name: get_entry_expired
get_entry_expired.doc:
Return expired value of the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Entry Expired`    | ${entry}                |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_entry_expires.name: get_entry_expires
get_entry_expires.doc:

get_entry_url.doc:

remove_entry_custom_property.name: remove_entry_custom_property
remove_entry_custom_property.doc:
Removes a custom property matching the given key of the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =                     | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Remove Entry Custom Property` | ${entry}               | foobar_attribute        |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_accessed_time.name: set_entry_accessed_time
set_entry_accessed_time.doc:
Sets accessed time of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${entry} =                | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry              | first=True |
        | ${value} =	            | Convert Date	         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Entry Accessed Time` | ${entry}               | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_entry_created_time.name: set_entry_created_time
set_entry_created_time.doc:
Sets created time of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |
        Example:
        | ${entry} =               | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry              | first=True |
        | ${value} =	           | Convert Date           | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Entry Created Time` | ${entry}               | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_entry_custom_property.name: set_entry_custom_property
set_entry_custom_property.doc:
Sets property ``key`` of the supplied ``entry`` to ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Custom Property` | new_field_name         | new_field_value         |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_expires.name: set_entry_expires
set_entry_expires.doc:
Sets expires value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =          | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Expires` | ${entry}               | True                    |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_expiry_time.name: set_entry_expiry_time
set_entry_expiry_time.doc:
Sets expiry time of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${entry} =              | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry              | first=True |
        | ${value} =	          | Convert Date	       | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Entry Expiry Time` | ${entry}               | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_entry_icon.name: set_entry_icon
set_entry_icon.doc:
Set the icon value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =        | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Icon`  | 20                                  | #Gear icon |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_modified_time.doc:
Sets modified time of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``entry``    | A valid KeePass entry                    |

        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${entry} =                | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry              | first=True |
        | ${value} =	            | Convert Date	         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Entry Modified Time` | ${entry}               | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_entry_notes.name: set_entry_notes
set_entry_notes.doc:
Set the notes value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =        | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Notes` | New\nnotes                                      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_password.name: set_entry_password
set_entry_password.doc:
Set the Password value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =           | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Password` | N3w Passw0rd                                     |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_tags.name: set_entry_tags
set_entry_tags.doc:
Set the tags value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | @{tags}=       | Create List | tag1     | tag2 |
        | Set Entry Tags | ${entry}   | ${tags}   | |
        | Set Entry Tags | ${entry}   | tag1;tag2 | |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_title.name: set_entry_title
set_entry_title.doc:
Set the title value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =        | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Title` | New Title                                        |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_url.name: set_entry_url
set_entry_url.doc:
Set the URL value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =      | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Url` | https://keepass.info/                            |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_entry_username.name: set_entry_username
set_entry_username.doc:
Set the username value of the supplied KeePass ``entry`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${entry} =           | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Set Entry Username` | New Username                                     |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

touch_entry.name: touch_entry
touch_entry.doc:
Touch the supplied KeePass ``entry``.

        | =Parameter= | =Description=         |
        | ``entry``   | A valid KeePass entry |
        | ``modify``  | Update modified time  |

        Example:
        | ${entry} =    | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        | `Touch Entry` | ${entry}               | False                   |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_entries.name: get_entries
get_entries.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given arguments.


        The ``history`` argument can be set to ``True`` to include history entries in the search, default value is False.

        The ``first`` argument can be set ``True`` this has the effect that only the first match will be returned, default value is False.

        The ``recursive`` argument can be set ``True`` this enables recursive searching, default value is False.

        The ``path`` argument sets the path which the entries should match, default value is None.

        The ``group`` argument has to match an existing Group is supplied only entries which are a direct child will be searched, default value is None. See ``Get Groups`` for information about selecting a group 

        See the `Entries and Groups` section for more information about Entries and Groups.


        *Additional arguments:*
        - The ``regex`` argument can be set to ``True`` this enables regular expression searching, default value is False.
        - The ``flags`` argument can be set to modify the regular expression search, default value is None. See the `Regular expression` section for more information about about the ``regex`` and ``flags`` syntax.
        - The ``title`` argument can be given to search matching titles, default value is None.
        - The ``username`` argument sets the username which the entries should match, default value is None.
        - The ``password`` argument sets the password which the entries should match, default value is None.
        - The ``url`` argument sets the url which the entries should match, default value is None.
        - The ``notes`` argument sets the notes which the entries should match, default value is None.
        - The ``uuid`` argument sets the uuid which the entries should match, default value is None.

        Examples:
        | @{entries}= | `Get Entries` | title=.*entry | regex=True |
        | ${entry}=   | `Get Entries` | title=.*entry | regex=True | first=True |
        | @{entries}= | `Get Entries` | title=.*entry | username=.*user     | regex=True |
        | @{entries}= | `Get Entries` | title=.*entry | notes=.*entry notes | regex=True |

        | ${group}=   | Get Groups By Name      | subgroup | first=True |
        | ${entries}= | Get Entries By Username | foobar   | group=${group} | first=True |

get_entries_all.name: get_entries_all
get_entries_all.doc:
Return a list of all entries in the open KeePass database.


        See the `Entries and Groups` section for more information about Entries and Groups.


        Example:
        | ${entries} = | `Get Entries All` |

get_entries_by_notes.name: get_entries_by_notes
get_entries_by_notes.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``notes``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | @{entries} = | `Get Entries By Notes` | root entry notes |

get_entries_by_password.name: get_entries_by_password
get_entries_by_password.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``password``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | @{entries} = | `Get Entries By Password` | passw0rd |

get_entries_by_path.name: get_entries_by_path
get_entries_by_path.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``path``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Note, only 1 entry can be selected by path

        Example:
        | ${entry} = | Get Entries By Path | foobar_group/group_entry |

get_entries_by_string.name: get_entries_by_string
get_entries_by_string.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``string``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | &{string}= | Create Dictionary | UserName=foobar_user | Title=group_entry |

        Valid dictonary keys:
        | Title |
        | UserName |
        | Password |
        | URL |
        | Notes |
        | IconID |
        | Tags |
        | History |

get_entries_by_title.name: get_entries_by_title
get_entries_by_title.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``title``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | @{entries} = | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry |
        => all entries with title: root_entry

        | ${entry} =   | `Get Entries By Title` | root_entry | first=True |
        => first entry with title: root_entry

get_entries_by_url.name: get_entries_by_url
get_entries_by_url.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``url``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | @{entries} = | `Get Entries By Url` | http://example.com |

get_entries_by_username.name: get_entries_by_username
get_entries_by_username.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``username``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | @{entries} = | `Get Entries By Username` | foobar_user |

get_entries_by_uuid.name: get_entries_by_uuid
get_entries_by_uuid.doc:
Return a list of entries in the open KeePass database matching the given ``uuid``.


        See `Get Entries` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | ${entries} = | Get Entries By Uuid | 12345678-1234-5678-1234-567812345678 |

get_group_accessed_time.name: get_group_accessed_time
get_group_accessed_time.doc:
Return accessed time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
        object of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name`      | foobar_group | first=True   |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Accessed Time` | ${group}     | timezone=UTC |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_group_created_time.name: get_group_created_time
get_group_created_time.doc:
Return created time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]

get_group_entries.name: get_group_entries
get_group_entries.doc:
Returns a list of entries from the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Entries`  | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_expired.name: get_group_expired
get_group_expired.doc:
Return expired value of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_expires.doc:
Return expires of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |

get_group_expiry_time.name: get_group_expiry_time
get_group_expiry_time.doc:
Return expiry time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]

        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |

get_group_icon.name: get_group_icon
get_group_icon.doc:
Return the icon of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

           Example:
           | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
           | ${value} = | `Get Group Icon`     | ${group}                  |

           New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_is_root_group.name: get_group_is_root_group
get_group_is_root_group.doc:
Return is root group value of the supplied KeePass ``group`.`

        Example:

get_group_modified_time.name: get_group_modified_time
get_group_modified_time.doc:
Return modified time as python
        [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
        object of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name`      | foobar_group | first=True   |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Modified Time` | ${group}     | timezone=UTC |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_group_name.name: get_group_name
get_group_name.doc:
Return the name value of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Name`     | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_notes.name: get_group_notes
get_group_notes.doc:
Return the notes value of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Notes`    | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_parent.name: get_group_parent
get_group_parent.doc:
Return the parentgroup of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Parent`   | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_path.name: get_group_path
get_group_path.doc:
Return the path value of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Path`     | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_subgroups.name: get_group_subgroups
get_group_subgroups.doc:
Returns a list of subgroups from the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name`  | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Subgroups` | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

get_group_uuid.name: get_group_uuid
get_group_uuid.doc:
Return the uuid of the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Uuid`     | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

group_should_be_expired.name: group_should_be_expired
group_should_be_expired.doc:
Fails if the specified group is not expired.

        Example:
        | ${group} =              | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | Set Group Expires       | ${group}             | ${TRUE}                   |
        | Group Should Be Expired | ${group}                                         |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

group_should_not_be_expired.name: group_should_not_be_expired
group_should_not_be_expired.doc:
Fails if the specified group is expired.

        Example:
        | ${group} =                  | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | Set Group Expires           | ${group}             | ${FALSE}                  |
        | Group Should Not Be Expired | ${group}                                         |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_group_accessed_time.name: set_group_accessed_time
set_group_accessed_time.doc:
Sets accessed time of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} =                | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group            | first=True |
        | ${value} =	            | Convert Date	       | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Group Accessed Time` | ${entry}             | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_group_created_time.name: set_group_created_time
set_group_created_time.doc:
Sets created time of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} =               | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group            | first=True |
        | ${value} =	           | Convert Date         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Group Created Time` | ${entry}             | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_group_expires.name: set_group_expires
set_group_expires.doc:
Sets expires value of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${group} =         | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | `Set Group Parent` | ${group}             | True                      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_group_expiry_time.name: set_group_expiry_time
set_group_expiry_time.doc:
Sets expiry time of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} =              | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group            | first=True |
        | ${value} =	          | Convert Date         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Group Expiry Time` | ${entry}             | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_group_icon.name: set_group_icon
set_group_icon.doc:
Set the ``icon`` of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

           Example:
           | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
           | `Set Group Expires`  | ${group}   | 20           | #Gear icon |

           New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_group_modified_time.name: set_group_modified_time
set_group_modified_time.doc:
Sets modified time of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        See the `Date and Time` section for more information about Date en Time.


        | =Parameter=  | =Description=                            |
        | ``group``    | A valid KeePass group                    |
        | ``value``    | A valid DateTime object                  |
        | ``timezone`` | A valid timezone string 'UTC' or 'local' |

        Example:
        | ${group} =                | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group            | first=True |
        | ${value} =	            | Convert Date         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
        | `Set Group modifeid Time` | ${entry}             | ${value}                | local      |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

set_group_name.name: set_group_name
set_group_name.doc:
Set the name value of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``

        Example:
        | ${group} =         | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | `Set Group Parent` | ${group}             | new_group_name            |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

set_group_notes.name: set_group_notes
set_group_notes.doc:
Set the notes value of the supplied KeePass ``group`` to the given ``value``.

        Example:
        | ${group} = | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | ${value} = | `Get Group Notes`    | ${group}                  |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.3

touch_group.name: touch_group
touch_group.doc:
Touch the supplied KeePass ``group``.

        | =Parameter= | =Description=            |
        | ``modify``  | update the modified time |

        Example:
        | ${group} =    | `Get Groups By Name` | foobar_group | first=True |
        | `Touch Group` | ${group}             | False                     |

        New in KeePassLibrary 0.8

get_groups.name: get_groups
get_groups.doc:
Return a list of groups in the open KeePass database matching the given arguments.

        The ``recursive`` argument can be set ``True`` this enables recursive searching, default value is False.

        The ``path`` argument sets the path which the groups should match, default value is None.

        The ``group`` argument has to match an existing Group is supplied only entries which are a direct child will be searched, default value is None. See ``Get Groups`` for information about selecting a group 

        See the `Entries and Groups` section for more information about Entries and Groups.


        *Additional arguments:*
        - The ``history`` argument can be set to ``True`` to include history groups in the search, default value is False.
        - The ``first`` argument can be set ``True`` this has the effect that only the first match will be returned, default value is False.
        - The ``regex`` argument can be set to ``True`` this enables regular expression searching, default value is False.
        - The ``flags`` argument can be set to modify the regular expression search, default value is None. See the `Regular expression` section for more information about about the ``regex`` and ``flags`` syntax.
        - The ``name`` argument can be given to search matching names, default value is None.
        - The ``notes`` argument sets the notes which the groups should match, default value is None.
        - The ``uuid`` argument sets the uuid which the groups should match, default value is None.

        Example:
        | @{groups}= | `Get Groups` | name=.*group | notes=^.{0}$ | regex=True              |
        | ${groups}= | `Get Groups` | name=.*group | notes=^.{0}$ | regex=True | first=True |

        | ${group}=   | `Get Groups By Name` | subgroup  | first=True     |
        | @{groups}= |  `Get Groups`         | subgroup2 | group=${group} |

get_groups_all.name: get_groups_all
get_groups_all.doc:
Return a list of all groups in the open KeePass database.

        See the `Entries and Groups` section for more information about Entries and Groups.


        Example:
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups All |

get_groups_by_name.name: get_groups_by_name
get_groups_by_name.doc:
Return a list of groups in the open KeePass database
        matching the given string.

        See `Get Groups` for more details about optional arguments.

        Examples:
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups By Name | subgroup              |
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups By Name | .*       | regex=True |

get_groups_by_notes.name: get_groups_by_notes
get_groups_by_notes.doc:
Return a list of groups in the open KeePass database
        matching the given notes.

        See `Get Groups` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups By Notes | group notes |

get_groups_by_path.name: get_groups_by_path
get_groups_by_path.doc:
Return a list of groups in the open KeePass database
        matching the given path.

        See `Get Groups` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups By Path | foobar_group/subgroup |

get_groups_by_uuid.name: get_groups_by_uuid
get_groups_by_uuid.doc:
Return a list of groups in the open KeePass database
        matching the given uuid.

        See `Get Groups` for more details about optional arguments.

        Example:
        | ${groups} = | Get Groups By Uuid | 12345678-1234-5678-1234-567812345678 |

__init__.name: __init__
__init__.doc:
KeePassLibrary is a library for Robot Framework.

KeePassLibrary uses the PyKeePass modules internally to access KeePass databases

See https://keepass.info for more information about KeePass in general.

== Table of contents ==

%TOC%

= Databases =

The following databases are supported;
- KDBX3 (3.1)
- KDBX4 (4.0)

https://keepass.info/help/kb/kdbx_4.html

Example:
| `Load Keepass Database` | Database.kdbx | Database.key | #Load a Keepass database named Database.kdbx using the keyfile Database.key |

= Entries and Groups =

A KeePass database (KDBX) is a tree of Groups, each Goup can contain multiple Groups and Entries

== Entry ==

Entries can be found using the `Get Entries` like keywords to return a single Entry or list of Entries.

| = Attribute = |
| title         |
| username      |
| password      |
| url           |
| tags          |
| icon          |
| parent_group  |
| uuid          |
| expires       |
| expired       |
| path          |

== Group ==

Groups can be found using the `Get Groups` like keywords to return a single Group or list of Groups.
| = Attribute = |
| name          |
| notes         |
| entries       |
| subgroups     |
| is_root_group |
| icon          |
| parent_group  |
| uuid          |
| expires       |
| expired       |
| path          |

= Regular expression =

When ``regex`` is set to True the supplied string is matched using regular expression:
- https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html#re-syntax
- https://docs.python.org/3/howto/regex.html#regex-howto

== Flags ==

When ``regex`` is set to True a combination of ``flags`` can be set:
| =Flag= | =Short Desciption= | =Long description= |
| i      | Ignore case        | Perform case-insensitive matching. |
| m      | Multiline          | Make begin/end {^, $} consider each line. |
| s      | Dotall             | Makes the '.' special character match any character at all, including a newline; without this flag, '.' will match anything except a newline. |
| l      | Locale             | Make {\w, \W,  , \B} follow locale. |
| x      | Verbose            | Allow comment in regex. |

= Date and Time =

Python's standard
[https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
objects are used both in input and output.

One nice benefit with datetime objects is that they have different time
components available as attributes that can be easily accessed using the
extended variable syntax.

Examples:
| ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry | first=True  |
| ${datetime} =               | `Get Entry Created Time` | ${entry}   | UTC         |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.year}         | 2017                     |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.month}        | 3                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.day}          | 13                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.hour}         | 1                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.minute}       | 8                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.second}       | 46                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.microsecond}  | 0                        |
| Should Be Equal As Strings  |     ${datetime.tzinfo}       | UTC                      |

By default KeePassLibrary threats datetime values when no tzinfo is present as UTC.
Examples:
- local timezone W. Europe Standard Time:
| ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry | first=True  |
| ${datetime} =               | `Get Entry Created Time` | ${entry}   | local       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.year}         | 2017                     |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.month}        | 3                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.day}          | 13                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.hour}         | 2                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.minute}       | 8                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.second}       | 46                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.microsecond}  | 0                        |
| Should Be Equal As Strings  |     ${datetime.tzinfo}       | W. Europe Standard Time  |

- local timezone W. Europe Daylight Time:
| ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry              | first=True |
| ${value} =                      | Convert Date             | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
| Set Entry Created Time      | ${entry}                 | ${value}                | local      |
| ${mtime}=                   | Get Entry Created Time   | ${entry}                | UTC        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.year}                | 2014                                 |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.month}               | 6                                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.day}                 | 11                                   |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.hour}                | 8                                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.minute}              | 07                                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.second}              | 42                                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.microsecond}     | 0                                        |
| Should Be Equal As Strings  | ${mtime.tzinfo}          | UTC                                      |
| ${mtime}=                   | Get Entry Created Time   | ${entry}                | local      |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.year}                | 2014                                     |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.month}               | 6                                        |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.day}                 | 11                                   |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.hour}                | 10                                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.minute}              | 07                                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.second}              | 42                                       |
| Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.microsecond}         | 0                                    |
| Should Be Equal As Strings  | ${mtime.tzinfo}          | W. Europe Daylight Time              |

__intro__.name: __intro__
__intro__.doc:
KeePassLibrary is a library for Robot Framework.

    KeePassLibrary uses the PyKeePass modules internally to access KeePass databases

    See https://keepass.info for more information about KeePass in general.

    == Table of contents ==

    %TOC%

    = Databases =

    The following databases are supported;
    - KDBX3 (3.1)
    - KDBX4 (4.0)

    https://keepass.info/help/kb/kdbx_4.html

    Example:
    | `Load Keepass Database` | Database.kdbx | Database.key | #Load a Keepass database named Database.kdbx using the keyfile Database.key |

    = Entries and Groups =

    A KeePass database (KDBX) is a tree of Groups, each Goup can contain multiple Groups and Entries

    == Entry ==

    Entries can be found using the `Get Entries` like keywords to return a single Entry or list of Entries.

    | = Attribute = |
    | title         |
    | username      |
    | password      |
    | url           |
    | tags          |
    | icon          |
    | parent_group  |
    | uuid          |
    | expires       |
    | expired       |
    | path          |

    == Group ==

    Groups can be found using the `Get Groups` like keywords to return a single Group or list of Groups.
    | = Attribute = |
    | name          |
    | notes         |
    | entries       |
    | subgroups     |
    | is_root_group |
    | icon          |
    | parent_group  |
    | uuid          |
    | expires       |
    | expired       |
    | path          |

    = Regular expression =

    When ``regex`` is set to True the supplied string is matched using regular expression:
    - https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html#re-syntax
    - https://docs.python.org/3/howto/regex.html#regex-howto

    == Flags ==

    When ``regex`` is set to True a combination of ``flags`` can be set:
    | =Flag= | =Short Desciption= | =Long description= |
    | i      | Ignore case        | Perform case-insensitive matching. |
    | m      | Multiline          | Make begin/end {^, $} consider each line. |
    | s      | Dotall             | Makes the '.' special character match any character at all, including a newline; without this flag, '.' will match anything except a newline. |
    | l      | Locale             | Make {\w, \W,  , \B} follow locale. |
    | x      | Verbose            | Allow comment in regex. |

    = Date and Time =

    Python's standard
    [https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime|datetime]
    objects are used both in input and output.

    One nice benefit with datetime objects is that they have different time
    components available as attributes that can be easily accessed using the
    extended variable syntax.

    Examples:
    | ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry | first=True  |
    | ${datetime} =               | `Get Entry Created Time` | ${entry}   | UTC         |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.year}         | 2017                     |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.month}        | 3                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.day}          | 13                       |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.hour}         | 1                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.minute}       | 8                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.second}       | 46                       |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.microsecond}  | 0                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Strings  |	${datetime.tzinfo}       | UTC                      |

    By default KeePassLibrary threats datetime values when no tzinfo is present as UTC.
    Examples:
    - local timezone W. Europe Standard Time:
    | ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry | first=True  |
    | ${datetime} =               | `Get Entry Created Time` | ${entry}   | local       |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.year}         | 2017                     |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.month}        | 3                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.day}          | 13                       |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.hour}         | 2                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.minute}       | 8                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.second}       | 46                       |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${datetime.microsecond}  | 0                        |
    | Should Be Equal As Strings  |	${datetime.tzinfo}       | W. Europe Standard Time  |

    - local timezone W. Europe Daylight Time:
    | ${entry} =                  | `Get Entries By Title`   | root_entry              | first=True |
    | ${value} =	              | Convert Date	         | 2014-06-11 10:07:42.123 | datetime   |
    | Set Entry Created Time      | ${entry}                 | ${value}                | local      |
    | ${mtime}=                   | Get Entry Created Time   | ${entry}                | UTC        |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.year}	         | 2014	                                |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.month}	         | 6		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.day}	         | 11	                                |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.hour}	         | 8		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.minute}	         | 07		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.second}	         | 42		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.microsecond}     | 0 	                                |
    | Should Be Equal As Strings  | ${mtime.tzinfo}          | UTC	                                |
    | ${mtime}=                   | Get Entry Created Time   | ${entry}                | local      |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.year}	         | 2014		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.month}	         | 6		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.day}	         | 11	                                |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.hour}	         | 10		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.minute}	         | 07		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.second}	         | 42		                            |
    | Should Be Equal As Integers | ${mtime.microsecond}	 | 0 	                                |
    | Should Be Equal As Strings  | ${mtime.tzinfo}          | W. Europe Daylight Time	            |
